title: Kaitiakitanga o Te Awa: Guardianship of the Mangakōtukutuku Stream
subject: English
culturalContext: Students will explore kaitiakitanga through the lens of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku, the stream that flows through our rohe and gives our college its name. They will engage with local pūrākau (stories) of the stream, learn about its historical significance to Ngāti Kahungunu, and examine contemporary environmental challenges facing this precious waterway.
learningObjectives: Analyze and interpret oral histories and written texts about Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku to identify themes of guardianship and environmental responsibility
Create a persuasive written piece advocating for the protection of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku, incorporating both mātauranga Māori and scientific evidence
Collaborate effectively in a group to research, plan, and deliver an oral presentation on a specific aspect of the stream's health and cultural significance
Evaluate different perspectives on environmental management of local waterways and articulate a personal position grounded in both cultural understanding and critical analysis

activities.title: Whakarongo: Listening to the Waters
activities.description: Students will visit Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku with local kaumātua who will share pūrākau and historical accounts of the stream. They will record key phrases, metaphors, and cultural concepts related to water guardianship, creating a glossary of terms that bridge Te Reo Māori and English understandings of kaitiakitanga.

activities.title: Titiro: Observing Through Multiple Lenses
activities.description: Working with science department equipment, students will conduct water quality testing while simultaneously documenting cultural observations. They will then write comparative analyses examining what scientific data reveals versus what cultural indicators (presence of native species, water clarity, etc.) show about the stream's health.

activities.title: Kōrero: Voices of the Community
activities.description: Students will interview diverse community members (farmers, local iwi representatives, council environmental officers) about their perspectives on the stream's management. They will transcribe and analyze these interviews to identify common concerns, conflicting priorities, and potential solutions.

activities.title: Tuhituhi: Writing for Change
activities.description: Using their research, students will craft persuasive letters to local council or editorials for the Hawke's Bay Today newspaper advocating for specific protection measures for Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku, effectively blending emotional appeal, cultural reasoning, and scientific evidence.

activities.title: Whakaatu: Presentation of Learning
activities.description: Groups will create multimedia presentations showcasing their findings and recommendations, to be presented to an audience including community stakeholders, using both English and incorporating appropriate Te Reo Māori terminology throughout.

resources: Ngāti Kahungunu kaumātua knowledge holders for oral histories
Hawke's Bay Regional Council 'State of the Environment' reports and water quality data
Local text: 'Te Awa Atua - The River of Life' by Ngāhuru Armstrong (Ngāti Kahungunu author)
Digital recording equipment for interviews and water testing kits from science department

tasks: Persuasive writing piece (letter or editorial) advocating for stream protection
Group multimedia presentation on research findings
Annotated bibliography of research sources including both Western and Māori perspectives
Reflective journal documenting personal learning journey about kaitiakitanga

nzcAlignment: English: Processes and Strategies - Integrating sources of information, processes, and strategies to identify, form, and express ideas
English: Ideas - Show understanding of ideas within, across, and beyond texts
English: Language Features - Use a range of language features appropriately for effect
English: Structure - Organize texts using appropriate conventions
Social Sciences: Understand how people manage resources and respond to challenges
